acceptance_criteria: Workbook parses with openpyxl and formulas are visible in the 'Formulas_Audit' sheet or formulas export.
Memo includes explicit cell references for all cited numeric results.
Scoring appendix documents rubric aggregation so final_score is reproducible from rubric_item_scores.
Verification note confirms steps and includes updated artifact hashes.
risk_mitigation_steps: Make only additive changes (new sheets and a brief memo appendix) to avoid altering the numeric outputs; when a formula must replace a hard-coded value, verify computed value matches previous value exactly.
Keep original workbook saved as a versioned file (e.g., alarm_fatigue_2_v1.xlsx) and submit a revised workbook (alarm_fatigue_2_v2.xlsx) so auditors can compare.

planned_changes.description: Add a 'Formulas_Audit' worksheet that lists each computed cell used in the memo (labels, formula text, and source cell references). Include formulas as text (prefixed with '=') so openpyxl and other tools can parse them.

Create an 'Audit' sheet that documents the canonical budget cell and all cost cells with explicit addresses (e.g., 'Inputs!B2 = Budget', 'Options!C4 = O1_cost'), and change any hard-coded numbers in result tables to be formula-driven references to those cells.

Update expert_memo.md to include explicit cell references for key numeric claims and tables (e.g., 'See Formulas_Audit!B5' or 'Impact!C8'), and add a short scoring appendix that documents the rubric aggregation method (weights, rounding rules).

Produce a formulas-only export (CSV or TXT) containing every formula used in the workbook and include it in the attempt artifacts to ensure automated verifiers can inspect formula text even if an environment lacks certain workbook tooling.

Run a local verification checklist: (a) open the workbook with openpyxl (or equivalent) to confirm formulas parse, (b) re-run sensitivity checks to ensure results unchanged, (c) confirm budget math references the budget cell and computes remaining budget as described.
Attach a short 'verification note' to the revised submission listing tests performed (openpyxl parse success, file hashes, and re-run summary outputs) and re-submit artifacts.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Address verifier warnings: make spreadsheet formulas inspectable and document score aggregation to enable independent verification; add explicit memo-to-sheet cell references for traceability.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Address verifier warnings: make spreadsheet formulas inspectable and document score aggregation to enable independent verification; add explicit memo-to-sheet cell references for traceability.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The baseline submission provides the required memo and workbook, documents inputs, methods, findings, sensitivity checks, and clear recommendations. Verifier and grader confirm artifact presence and general semantic grounding. Two medium-severity issues remain: (1) the workbook could not be parsed by the verifier (openpyxl unavailable) so formula-level reproducibility could not be independently verified; and (2) the grader's numeric final_score (0.915) is not accompanied by an explicit aggregation method for rubric-item scores, preventing independent recomputation of the score. These are addressable with small, targeted additions to the deliverables and brief clarifying documentation.
